envelope_defaults.human_readable_summary: Jira / Linear project tracker readiness substrate per ADR-0084. Wave 6 matrix score 12.75 (Project / Engineering TIER_1). Two-vendor catalog item: Jira Cloud + Linear.
envelope_defaults.model_usage_notes: Suggest-only. Activation requires Foundation governance + map-region approval + per-connector RULE 21 research arc completion. Jira and Linear treated as a coordinated pair given common downstream workflow purpose bindings.

items.MCP_posture: Atlassian official MCP server emerging; substrate-honest evaluation deferred to C4 pre-flight. Initial path is direct REST API via OAuth 2.0 3LO classic scopes.
items.OAuth_model: OAuth 2.0 3LO (3-legged OAuth) for Cloud apps. Classic scopes recommended over granular scopes; keep total under 50 per Atlassian best practice. Forge apps for marketplace distribution; direct OAuth 2.0 3LO for direct integration.
items.app_installation_model: Atlassian Developer Console app + OAuth 2.0 3LO client. Marketplace listing for org-wide install. Cloud app installed per-site (Jira Cloud instance).
items.admin_consent_model: Site admin consents to app at OAuth flow / Marketplace install. Per-user grants captured at OAuth; site admin can revoke app site-wide.
read_capabilities: read:jira-work (read issues / projects / boards — classic scope)
write_capabilities: write:jira-work (create / update issues — disabled by default)
manage:jira-project (project management — disabled by default; admin-tier)
manage:jira-configuration (workflow / scheme changes — disabled by default)
webhook_or_event_capabilities: Jira webhooks for issue / sprint / version / worklog events
Webhook signature verification via Atlassian-provided HMAC secret + verifyInboundHmac substrate
Webhooks subscribed by app or by user with manage:jira-configuration
required_approval_gates: Map-region approval per ADR-0082 Amendment 1 (Tool Map + Workflow Map must approve Jira)
Admin policy approval at activation per ADR-0084
Per-call governance approval for any write action
dual_control_recommendations: Bulk issue writes (>20 issues / window) — second-actor approval
User-impact writes (assignee / reporter changes on >10 issues) — admin notification
DMW_scope_implications: Issue / project metadata scoped to ENTERPRISE DMW per Wave 2.1 §25 (engineering / project domain knowledge)
Sprint / board state scoped to team-scoped DMW
User-PII (assignee identity, reporter identity) treated as same-org-only — never cross-tenant
Comments / descriptions treated as project-scoped DMW; sensitive projects excluded by Risk Map
workflow_purpose_bindings: ADR-0081 Stage 2: Sprint risk summary recommendations
ADR-0081 Stage 3: Drafted issue updates / comments awaiting human approval
ADR-0081 Stage 3: Cross-tool linkage proposals (Jira ↔ GitHub PRs / Slack threads)
items.billing_pack_mapping: Project / Engineering Pack family per ADR-0083 Amendment 1 §9.4 (connector-pack.project-engineering.v1).
Dandelion_map_dependencies: Workflow Map must approve Jira-bound workflows (sprint / triage / release-readiness)
Authority Map must approve issue-write authority + project-admin authority
Memory / DMW Map must approve project / sprint memory scope
Risk Map must clear sensitive projects excluded (M&A / security / legal projects)
audit_expectations: Per-action point-cost surfaced in audit metadata (Jira points-based rate limit per March 2026 enforcement)
secret_handling_requirements: OAuth client_id / client_secret per tenant via secret_ref env-var-NAMEs per ADR-0024 + ADR-0019
Per-user refresh tokens stored encrypted at ConnectorBinding row; never in logs
Webhook HMAC secret per tenant via secret_ref
no_leak_rules: Issue content / description / comments never traverse responses outside the wallet's scope
Reporter / assignee user identity never exposed cross-tenant
Project keys / issue keys never exposed cross-tenant
tenant_isolation_requirements: Per-tenant Atlassian Cloud site (Jira Cloud is site-isolated by construction)
ConnectorBinding enforces wallet_id + entity scope on every API call
items.rate_limit_notes: Points-based + tiered quota rate limits enforced from March 2, 2026 (per Atlassian Developer docs). Each API call consumes points based on operation cost. Implementation must surface point-cost in audit metadata + back off on 429.
testing_strategy: Fixture-based read tests against FixtureBasedConnectorAdapter pattern
Replay-mode integration tests using recorded Jira responses
implementation_risks: March 2026 rate-limit enforcement — must be points-aware from day one
Confused-deputy via 3LO: per-user token impersonation scope must be enforced in audit
Scope sprawl: keep <50 scopes per Atlassian best practice
Webhook delivery best-effort — replay strategy required
items.first_slice_recommendation: C4-A Jira Cloud read-first connector runtime — issue / project / board read via OAuth 2.0 3LO classic scopes. NO writes at C4-A. Webhook ingestion via verifyInboundHmac. After C2 Slack + C3 Google Workspace + GOVSEC.6.

items.MCP_posture: Linear has official MCP server. Substrate-honest evaluation deferred to C4-B pre-flight; MCP layer likely reduces governance surface vs. direct GraphQL integration.
items.OAuth_model: OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow. Scopes: read / write / admin / issues:create / comments:create. Personal API keys also available for individual-user automations (lower-trust path).
items.app_installation_model: Linear OAuth app at developer settings + workspace-level install. Linear is single-workspace per Linear org; per-tenant install pattern.
items.admin_consent_model: Workspace admin consents at OAuth install. Per-user token grants captured at install.
read_capabilities: read scope: issues / projects / cycles / teams / users / labels
Issue search + filter by team / cycle / state / assignee
write_capabilities: write scope: issue create / update / comment (disabled by default)
admin scope (project / team management — disabled by default)
webhook_or_event_capabilities: Linear webhooks for issue / comment / project / cycle events
Webhook signature header verification per Linear docs
Subscribe via developer settings or programmatically per-team
required_approval_gates: Admin policy approval at activation per ADR-0084
Per-call governance approval for any write action
dual_control_recommendations: Project / cycle structural changes — dual-control mandatory
DMW_scope_implications: Issue / project metadata scoped to ENTERPRISE DMW
Team / cycle state scoped to team-scoped DMW
Comments treated as project-scoped DMW; sensitive projects excluded by Risk Map
Linear is single-workspace per org so cross-org leakage risk is structurally lower than multi-tenant tools
workflow_purpose_bindings: ADR-0081 Stage 2: Sprint / cycle risk summary
ADR-0081 Stage 3: Drafted issue / comment updates awaiting approval
items.billing_pack_mapping: Project / Engineering Pack family per ADR-0083 Amendment 1 §9.4 (connector-pack.project-engineering.v1).
Dandelion_map_dependencies: Workflow Map must approve Linear-bound workflows
Authority Map must approve issue-write authority
Memory / DMW Map must approve Linear memory scope
Risk Map must clear sensitive projects
secret_handling_requirements: OAuth client_id / client_secret per tenant via secret_ref env-var-NAMEs per ADR-0024 + ADR-0019
Per-user OAuth tokens encrypted at ConnectorBinding row
Webhook signing secret per tenant via secret_ref
no_leak_rules: Issue content / comments never traverse responses outside the wallet's scope
Linear user identity never exposed cross-tenant
Issue identifiers (TEAM-NNN format) never exposed cross-tenant
tenant_isolation_requirements: ConnectorBinding enforces wallet_id + entity scope on every API call
items.rate_limit_notes: Linear uses GraphQL query complexity limits. Standard limit ~1500 complexity points per minute per OAuth client. Implementation should query selectively and use webhooks for delta.
testing_strategy: Replay-mode integration tests using recorded Linear responses
implementation_risks: GraphQL query complexity — must be measured + bounded
Linear MCP server option — evaluate before direct GraphQL to reduce surface
Personal API keys vs OAuth — prefer OAuth for governance + audit trail
items.first_slice_recommendation: C4-B Linear read-first connector runtime — issue / project / cycle GraphQL queries via OAuth 2.0. NO writes at C4-B. Evaluate Linear MCP server before adopting direct GraphQL. After Jira C4-A or in parallel.
